name: Italy
heroSummary: Public universities, regional scholarships, low living costs, and English-taught programmes make Italy our flagship destination.

highlights.title: Free education pathways
highlights.body: Public universities can make higher education feel much more accessible for international students.

highlights.title: Guaranteed scholarships up to €8,000
highlights.body: Regional scholarships like DSU can dramatically reduce costs and make the ROI much easier to manage.

highlights.title: English-taught and Europe-connected
highlights.body: A wide range of English-taught programmes and Schengen travel access make the student experience especially attractive.

requirements: Financial documents for the visa stage
scholarships: Regional Scholarships (Lazio Disco, EDISU Piemonte, DSU Toscana, Veneto)

faqs.q: How does the scholarship work?
faqs.a: All our students are eligible for the Regional Government Scholarship, which offers a 100% tuition fee waiver and a stipend for living expenses. It can make studying in Italy possible with no tuition fees and financial support for your stay.

faqs.q: Do I need to meet any specific academic requirements?
faqs.a: Yes. For courses in Italian public universities, you generally need at least 60% marks in your previous education to be eligible for most programs.

faqs.q: Is the IMAT exam required for MBBS programs?
faqs.a: Yes. The IMAT (International Medical Admissions Test) is compulsory for students applying to MBBS programs in Italy, and it must be taken before applying to medical schools.

faqs.q: Are the courses taught in English?
faqs.a: Yes. The Italian public universities we apply to offer programs in English, whether you are applying for a bachelor's or master's degree.

faqs.q: How does Go Study Overseas choose the universities for me?
faqs.a: We shortlist Italian public universities based on your academic profile, career goals, preferences, preferred course, and location so the final choice aligns with your aspirations.

faqs.q: What is included in the INR 1.4 lakh fee?
faqs.a: The package covers university applications for 4 to 5 choices, pre-enrollment assistance, scholarship application support, visa guidance, and pre-departure support. It does not include visa proof-of-funds, DOV charges, travel insurance, IELTS or other exam fees, or the VFS visa fee.

faqs.q: What documents are needed for the visa application?
faqs.a: You will need proof of financial funds for your stay in Italy, along with a valid passport, university admission letter, proof of scholarship, proof of funds, health insurance, and the visa application fee paid to VFS Global.

faqs.q: Can I apply for both bachelor’s and master’s programs through Go Study Overseas?
faqs.a: Yes. We assist students with both bachelor's and master's programmes, whether they are starting their higher education journey or continuing at postgraduate level.

faqs.q: What happens if I am not eligible for the scholarship?
faqs.a: If any eligibility issue comes up, our team helps resolve it or explores alternative solutions so you can still pursue financial support wherever possible.

faqs.q: Do you provide post-arrival services in Italy?
faqs.a: Our primary services focus on the application, scholarship, and visa process, but we also provide pre-departure assistance for accommodation, bank accounts, the residence permit, tax code, and cultural orientation. We do not provide direct on-ground post-arrival services in Italy.

faqs.q: How long does the entire application process take?
faqs.a: The full process, including application submission, scholarship assistance, and visa processing, generally takes around 6 to 8 months, so it is best to start early.

faqs.q: When can I apply for the September intake?
faqs.a: Preparations for the September intake are already underway. Deadlines vary by university, so the safest approach is to apply as early as possible to keep the process smooth.

faqs.q: Do I need to learn Italian to study in Italy?
faqs.a: No. The programs we assist with are taught in English, though learning some basic Italian can help with daily life and make the experience easier.

faqs.q: Can I work while studying in Italy?
faqs.a: Yes. International students can usually work up to 20 hours per week during the academic year and full-time during breaks, provided they have the necessary student visa.

faqs.q: What are the advantages of studying in Italy?
faqs.a: Italy offers high-quality education in world-renowned universities, strong scholarship support, English-taught options, rich cultural experiences, excellent healthcare, vibrant student life, low living costs compared with many European destinations, part-time work opportunities, a 1-year post-study visa route, and Schengen travel access.

name: Germany
heroSummary: Public universities, solid industry links, and globally respected technical programs make Germany a smart pick.

heroStats.label: Typical tuition
heroStats.value: Low or zero at public universities

highlights.title: A strong public university system
highlights.body: Germany is known for accessible public education and a practical approach to technical and research degrees.

highlights.title: Industry-ready outcomes
highlights.body: Its ecosystem is built around internships, labs, apprenticeships, and direct links to major employers.

highlights.title: A great fit for technical profiles
highlights.body: If your background is in engineering, computer science, or manufacturing, the fit can be exceptionally strong.

universities.name: IU International University of Applied Sciences

costs.label: Tuition
costs.value: Often very low at public universities

requirements: English language proof for taught programs
Proof of funds for the blocked account stage

faqs.q: Are public universities really low cost?
faqs.a: In many cases, yes. Tuition can be very low compared with other destinations, though living costs still need to be budgeted properly.

faqs.q: Do I need German for masters?
faqs.a: Not always. There are many English-taught master's programs, especially in engineering, STEM, and business.

faqs.q: What makes Germany attractive beyond cost?
faqs.a: Its combination of academic credibility, internship culture, and strong employment prospects after graduation is a major draw.

name: France
heroSummary: France blends strong institutions, cosmopolitan cities, and a wide range of business and policy programmes.

highlights.title: Brand-heavy education
highlights.body: French universities and grandes écoles can add serious weight to a CV, especially in business and management.

highlights.title: A polished international experience
highlights.body: The country is a strong choice for students looking for a global classroom with a vibrant city lifestyle.

highlights.title: Career-facing programmes
highlights.body: Many courses are designed with placement, project work, and industry partnerships in mind.

requirements: Academic records and statement of purpose
English proficiency proof for international programmes

faqs.q: Is France only for luxury or business students?
faqs.a: Not at all. Those are popular options, but there are also strong pathways in policy, engineering, design, and hospitality.

faqs.q: Can I study in English?
faqs.a: Yes. Many schools offer English-taught programmes, especially at postgraduate level.

faqs.q: How competitive are top schools?
faqs.a: Highly competitive. We help position your application with the right story, documents, and shortlisting strategy.

name: United Kingdom
heroSummary: Shorter master's degrees, major universities, and a strong global reputation make the UK a classic study destination.

highlights.title: One-year master's advantage
highlights.body: Many programmes are shorter than elsewhere, which can reduce your total time away from the workforce.

highlights.title: Brand recognition worldwide
highlights.body: UK universities are known globally and can be especially valuable for students aiming at international careers.

highlights.title: Huge variety of courses
highlights.body: From niche master's tracks to large research universities, the UK has a broad academic menu.

requirements: Financial documents for the visa process

faqs.q: Why do students choose the UK for masters?
faqs.a: The shorter course duration, strong brand value, and wide choice of programmes make it a powerful option.

faqs.q: Are scholarships available?
faqs.a: Yes. Scholarships vary by university and profile, and we help identify where you have the strongest shot.

faqs.q: How much time should I allow for the process?
faqs.a: A good plan gives you enough room for applications, funding documents, and the visa timeline without rushing.

name: Canada
heroSummary: Canada is a strong choice for students looking for quality education, work opportunities, and long-term migration pathways.

highlights.title: Co-op and work options
highlights.body: Many programmes include practical experience, which helps students build a stronger post-study profile.

highlights.title: Clear long-term pathway
highlights.body: For many students, Canada stands out because study, work, and migration pathways are easier to plan around.

highlights.title: A balanced student experience
highlights.body: The mix of academics, quality of life, and career opportunities is a big part of the appeal.

requirements: Degree relevant to your chosen programme
English proficiency proof for most institutions
Funds for tuition and living costs

faqs.q: Is Canada a good option for long-term planning?
faqs.a: It can be. Many students like the combination of education, work opportunities, and future pathway planning.

faqs.q: Do co-op programmes matter?
faqs.a: Yes. They can make a huge difference if you're focused on employability and practical experience.

faqs.q: Can I study multiple intakes?
faqs.a: Many universities offer more than one intake, but your profile and target programme will determine the best option.

name: USA
heroSummary: The USA offers unmatched variety, a strong research culture, and huge flexibility across universities and programmes.

highlights.title: An enormous choice set
highlights.body: The US is unmatched for the sheer number of universities, specialisations, and research possibilities.

highlights.title: Strong for research profiles
highlights.body: If you want laboratory work, publications, or technical depth, the US can be a very strong fit.

highlights.title: Flexibility across career paths
highlights.body: Students often like the ability to mix majors, electives, and internships in a very customised way.

universities.name: Drexel University
universities.program: MS in Robotics and Autonomous Systems

requirements: Standardised or English test scores where required
Financial documents for admission and visa filing

faqs.q: Is the US only for top-ranked applicants?
faqs.a: No. There are a huge range of universities, and the right fit depends on your budget, goals, and academic background.

faqs.q: Do assistantships matter?
faqs.a: They can make a huge difference in funding and experience if your programme offers them.

faqs.q: When should I start?
faqs.a: Early planning helps, especially if you need tests, funding documentation, and a balanced list of universities.

name: Australia
menuNote: Group of Eight and flexible intakes
heroSummary: Australia combines globally ranked universities, a friendly student culture, and a flexible intake calendar.

highlights.title: Flexible intake options
highlights.body: Multiple intake windows make it easier to align applications with your current academic timeline.

highlights.title: Strong campus culture
highlights.body: Australia is known for being student-friendly, practical, and easy to settle into.

highlights.title: Great for work-study balance
highlights.body: A lot of students like the balance of academic quality and part-time work opportunities.

requirements: Statement of purpose or application essay

faqs.q: Is Australia a good fit for flexible planning?
faqs.a: Yes. The intake calendar gives students more room to choose a timing that works for them.

faqs.q: Are scholarships available?
faqs.a: Merit and faculty awards do exist, though they depend heavily on the university and your profile.

faqs.q: What makes Australia appealing?
faqs.a: A strong academic brand, approachable student culture, and practical post-study options are the big draws.

name: Malta
heroTag: EU study with an English-speaking edge
heroSummary: GBS Malta brings a practical international study option in a compact, English-friendly European setting.

highlights.title: English widely used
highlights.body: Malta is a natural fit for students who want a European base without a language barrier.

highlights.title: Compact and easy to settle into
highlights.body: The island size keeps travel simple, and the student experience feels more manageable than a mega-city.

highlights.title: Internationally connected programs
highlights.body: A practical option for students looking for applied, career-facing programmes.

faqs.q: Is Malta a good fit for English-medium study?
faqs.a: Yes. Malta is attractive because English is widely used in study and daily life.

faqs.q: What type of student is this destination best for?
faqs.a: Students looking for an accessible European study environment with an international campus feel.

faqs.q: Can scholarships help reduce the total cost?
faqs.a: Yes. Institutional and partner support can help make the budget much more manageable.

name: Dubai
menuNote: Global business hub with international campuses
heroSummary: GBS Dubai offers an international, business-focused option in one of the region's fastest-growing education hubs.

highlights.title: Strong business and finance reputation
highlights.body: Dubai is a practical choice for students focused on applied business education and regional opportunities.

highlights.title: International student ecosystem
highlights.body: The city is built for global mobility, with campuses and employers that think internationally.

highlights.title: Fast-moving and career-oriented
highlights.body: A strong fit for students who want a modern study destination with direct industry exposure.

universities.name: Manipal Academy of Higher Education Dubai

faqs.q: Why do students choose Dubai?
faqs.a: It combines a global city experience with business-focused education and strong career visibility.

faqs.q: Is English enough for study?
faqs.a: For many international programmes, yes. English is the main language used in academic settings.

faqs.q: Are scholarships possible?
faqs.a: Yes. Early-bird and partner support can help reduce the overall fee burden.
